data.title: Korean Won Weakens to 17-Year Low Amid Mounting Growth Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: The Korean won weakens to a 17-year low against the dollar in Asian session amid mounting growth concerns spurred by the Middle East conflict. "Investors [are] placing greater weight on slowing growth rather than renewed inflation pressures from the energy shock," two FX strategists at OCBC Group Research say in a research report. The current round of selling is hitting pro-cyclical currencies such as the won, the strategists say. "There is a gradual shift in repricing of global growth risks as Iran conflict appears to last longer than expected, moving past a volatility event," they add. The dollar rises 1.1% to 1,534.40 won after earlier touching 1,536.80 won, its highest intraday level since 2009, LSEG data show.(ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bank of Korea Governor Nominee Says Dollar Liquidity Remains Ample
data.description:
By Jihye Lee

Bank of Korea governor nominee Shin Hyun-song said U.S. dollar liquidity in South Korea remains ample despite recent volatility in the won, suggesting external risks to the financial system are contained.

Speaking to reporters ahead of a parliamentary confirmation hearing on Tuesday, Shin said robust foreign inflows into the local bond market, particularly through foreign-exchange swaps, have helped underpin dollar funding conditions.

"Although the exchange rate is high, dollar liquidity is currently quite ample," he said. "In such cases, investors effectively lend dollars and borrow foreign currency, which means dollar funding is abundant."

"Stronger dollar liquidity means there is less need to link exchange-rate movements with financial-market stability as in the past," he said.

His comments come as the Korean won has come under pressure amid geopolitical uncertainties. Still, Shin, who is expected to assume the position of the head of the central bank mid-April, signaled confidence that domestic markets are well positioned to absorb volatility in the currency.

"The market is capable of absorbing risks related to exchange-rate fluctuations," he said.

The dollar was recently up 1.2% against the won at 1,535.30, according to LSEG data, marking the local currency's weakest level since 2009.

Write to Jihye Lee at jihye.lee@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Catalyst Media interim net loss narrows on lower SIS losses
data.description:
Overview

  • UK media investment firm's interim net loss narrowed yr/yr as SIS losses decreased

  • SIS revenues rose but racing market remains challenging, with headwinds expected in Q4

  • Company completed £90,051 share buyback and received £0.35 mln dividend from SIS

Outlook

  • Catalyst Media says SIS expects margin and rights headwinds to impact full-year results

  • Company expects SIS's non-racing business to continue growing with new customers and events

Result Drivers

  • RACING MARKET CHALLENGES - SIS said market conditions for racing remain challenging, with declining betting turnover in the UK impacting content revenues

  • NEW PRODUCTS - SIS launched two additional 49s numbers draws and a third esports title, eAmerican Football, which contributed to growth

data.title: UK's Flexion Mobile Q4 revenue falls 4% as influencer marketing weakens
data.description:
Overview

  • UK mobile games distributor's Q4 revenue fell 4% yr/yr as influencer marketing weakened

  • Distribution revenue grew 6% yr/yr, offsetting some decline in total revenue

  • Adjusted EBITDA turned positive, helped by cost controls and restructuring

Outlook

  • Flexion expects restructuring to conclude in Q3 2026, aiming for predictable profitability

  • Company sees market changes fuelling demand for services, citing Epic-Google settlement

  • Flexion anticipates improved efficiency as teams introduce AI and streamline processes

Result Drivers

  • DISTRIBUTION GROWTH - Core distribution revenue rose 6% yr/yr, driven by new game signings and growth in gaming distribution customers, per chairman Andrew Shen

  • MARKETING SERVICES DECLINE - Marketing services revenue fell 53% yr/yr due to weak influencer marketing and strategic realignment, including phasing out non-gaming operations

  • COST CONTROLS - Company said cost structure is trending down as core distribution revenue grows, aided by internal efficiency measures and restructuring

data.title: India's Zetwerk files confidentially for IPO draft papers
data.description:
India's Zetwerk has filed confidential draft papers with the market regulator for its initial public offering, a newspaper advertisement on Tuesday showed.

Earlier this month, Reuters reported that the contract electronics manufacturer was aiming to raise up to $550 million at a valuation of about $4 billion.

The company is targeting a listing later this year, the sources said, adding that the IPO includes about $300 million in fresh equity, with the remainder coming from an offer for sale.

In India, a confidential filing allows companies to submit draft IPO papers to the Securities and Exchange Board of India for review without making them public until they decide to proceed with the offering.

India was the world's second‑largest IPO market in 2025, LSEG data showed, but sentiment has weakened this year after a selloff in technology services stocks, with recent listings such as Fractal Analytics and Shadowfax Technologies struggling on debut.

Founded in 2018, Zetwerk supplies industries ranging from consumer electronics to aerospace and defence, with more than 10 facilities across India, the United States, Mexico and Europe. Its client base includes Samsung, Volvo and Honeywell.

data.title: UK's A G Barr yearly revenue slightly beats estimates on higher pricing, raises dividend
data.description:
Overview

  • UK soft drinks maker's fiscal year revenue rose 4%, slightly beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted pretax profit for the year slightly exceeded analyst expectations

  • Company raised full-year dividend by 11% after strong cash generation and acquisitions

Outlook

  • A G Barr expects low double-digit percentage revenue growth in FY26/27

  • Company aims for revenue growth ≥ 4% per yr, operating margin 14-16%, ROCE 19-21%

  • Company expects operational efficiencies from acquisitions to be realised from H2 FY26/27

Result Drivers

  • PRICING ACTIONS - Revenue growth was driven by price increases across core brands, though this led to short-term volume softness in some areas

  • OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY - Margin improvement was supported by operational efficiencies and disciplined cost management

data.title: UK's Raspberry Pi 2025 revenue rises 25% on strong demand
data.description:
Overview

  • UK computing hardware maker's 2025 revenue rose 25% on higher unit sales and ASPs

  • Adjusted EBITDA up 25%, driven by demand and improved unit economics

  • Adjusted EPS for 2025 grew 35% yr/yr

Outlook

  • Raspberry Pi expects FY 2026 profitability to be in-line with market estimates, revenue materially higher

  • Company sees strong sales momentum continuing into opening months of FY 2026

  • Raspberry Pi expects DRAM supply constraints to persist beyond this year, with mitigation from demand elasticity and foundry investment

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER-VALUE PRODUCT MIX - Growth in sales of higher-priced Raspberry Pi 4 and 5 boards, along with increased accessory sales, contributed to revenue and profit gains

  • STRONG DEMAND IN KEY MARKETS - Robust demand from OEMs and resellers, particularly in the US and China, supported higher unit shipments

  • SUPPLY CHAIN AND PRICING ACTIONS - Supplier diversification, pricing adjustments, and inventory buffers helped offset rising DRAM costs

data.title: ROHM Adds New Lineup of 17 High-performance Op Amps Enhancing Design Flexibility
data.description:
ROHM Co., Ltd./ Key word(s): Product Launch

ROHM Adds New Lineup of 17 High-performance Op Amps Enhancing Design Flexibility

31.03.2026 / 08:05 CET/CEST

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

KYOTO, Japan, March 31, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- ROHM Co., Ltd. has added the new CMOS Operational Amplifier (op amp) series "TLRx728" and "BD728x" to its lineup. These are suitable for a wide range of applications including automotive, industrial, and consumer systems. A broad lineup also makes product selection easier.

Figures: Product features

https://cdn.kyodonewsprwire.jp/prwfile/release/M106254/202603185852/_prw_PI1fl_12w7lWE6.jpg 

In recent years, demand for high-accuracy op amps has been rapidly increasing as automotive and industrial systems become more sophisticated, demanding faster speed, better precision, and higher efficiency. In applications requiring amplification of sensor outputs, minimizing signal error and delay is essential. To meet these requirements, a well-balanced set of key characteristics is needed, including input offset voltage, noise, and slew rate.

These new products are high-performance op amps that offer a low input offset voltage, low noise, and high slew rate. TLRx728 features an input offset voltage of 150 microvolts (typ.), while BD728x offers 1.6 millivolts (typ.). Both series have a noise voltage density of 12 nanovolts per square root hertz at 1kHz and a slew rate of 10 volts per microsecond. They are therefore suitable for a wide range of precision applications, including sensor signal processing, current detection circuits, motor driver control, and power supply monitoring systems. Both series are designed to balance versatility and high performance rather than being limited to specific applications.

Rail-to-rail input/output capability allows maximum utilization of the power supply voltage range, ensuring a wide dynamic range. Furthermore, in addition to 1 channel, 2 channels, and 4 channels configurations, a diverse range of packages is available, enabling optimal product selection based on application and board size.

The new products are being released simultaneously except for certain part numbers (Sample Price: 1-channel: $2.0, 2-channels: $2.8, 4-channels: $4.0 per unit, excluding tax). For key product characteristics, please refer to the lineup table. They are also available for purchase online through online distributors such as DigiKey, Mouser and Farnell.

Figures: key product lineup table

https://kyodonewsprwire.jp/attach/202603185852-O1-M2KnjTXU.pdf 

Application Examples

Automotive equipment, industrial equipment, and consumer electronics.

Example use case: Sensor signal processing, current detection circuits, motor driver control, and power supply monitoring systems.

News release: https://www.rohm.com/news-detail?news-title=2026-03-31_news_opamp&defaultGroupId=false 

About ROHM: https://kyodonewsprwire.jp/attach/202603185852-O2-p21y47fb.pdf 

Logo: https://cdn.kyodonewsprwire.jp/prwfile/release/M106254/202603185852/_prw_PI2fl_Qro6Es5C.jpg 

Official website: https://www.rohm.com/ 

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/de/pressemitteilungen/rohm-adds-new-lineup-of-17-high-performance-op-amps-enhancing-design-flexibility-302729343.html

data.title: Unilever in Advanced Talks to Combine Food Unit With McCormick
data.description:
By Aimee Look

Unilever said it is in advanced discussions over an agreement to combine its food business with spice maker McCormick, in a deal that would streamline its business around beauty, personal-care products and home.

The Wall Street Journal reported Monday that the companies were close to an agreement that would create a $60 billion food giant.

Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com

data.title: April 2026 Bank Holidays: Bank closures from Good Friday to Akshaya Tritiya; Check city-wise holiday list
data.description:
As the new financial year begins from April 1st, the monthly holiday calendar is here. Banks across India will observe several holidays in April 2026, with the exact dates differing from state to state due to regional festivals and local observances. In total, banks could remain closed for up to 14 days during the month, including weekends, second and fourth Saturdays, and officially declared public holidays.

Since the holiday schedule is not uniform nationwide, customers are advised to review their respective state-wise calendars in advance to avoid any inconvenience while planning visits to bank branches.

RBI holiday calendar for April 2026

As per the official holiday list issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), April will see multiple closures linked to key religious and cultural occasions. These include Maundy Thursday, Good Friday, Bohag Bihu, Bengali New Year's Day, and Akshaya Tritiya.

The holiday cycle begins with April 1st itself when all banks will remain shut for 'banks’ closing of accounts.' Rest of the holidays are under 'Negotiable Instruments Act'. This means that some states may see bank closure while other states may see normal functioning.

It will be followed by Good Friday on April 3. April 14 will witness several observances across different regions, including Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Jayanti, Maha Vishuva Sankranti, Bohag Bihu, and Baisakhi. Later in the month, Bohag Bihu will also be observed on April 16, followed by Basava Jayanti and Akshaya Tritiya on April 20.

Online banking services remain available

Apart from these holidays, banks remain shut on all Sundays as well as the second and fourth Saturdays of every month. The RBI classifies bank holidays under three categories: the Negotiable Instruments Act, RTGS holidays, and Banks' Closing of Accounts.

Customers are encouraged to plan their banking activities after checking local holiday schedules. However, essential digital services such as ATM withdrawals, net banking, and UPI transactions will continue to function even on bank holidays.

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on March 31
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events and financial market moves may affect South African markets on Tuesday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

April fuel price adjustments expected

Central bank quarterly bulletin

Fourth-quarter formal sector employment

February trade balance

Weekly government bond auction

President Ramaphosa addresses investment conference

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand weakened on Monday as the month-long war in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated, while markets are seeking clarity on talks between the U.S. and Iran.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Oil prices were set for a record monthly rise on Tuesday while Asian shares were headed for their steepest fall in six years, capping a tumultuous month as the war in the Middle East stoked fears of higher inflation and slower growth.

WALL STREET

Major U.S. stock indexes mostly fell on Monday as U.S. President Donald Trump's new warning to Tehran and a widening of the Middle East war offset optimism over his comments on U.S. discussions with Iran.

GOLD

Gold prices rose on Tuesday on hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East conflict, but were poised for their worst month in more than 17 years as higher energy prices dimmed hopes for a U.S. interest rate cut this year.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

BUSINESS DAY

- Treasury's power to raise fuel levy faces legal test

- Valterra chair slams mining law reform rollout

NEWS24

- No diesel at some fuel stations as pre-hike rush overwhelms logistics
